organization_information.complete_description_of_your_operations_:
Southside Youth Sports Alliance (SYSA) is a 501(c)(3) not-for-profit organization dedicated to providing affordable, community-based recreational and developmental sports programs for youth ages 6–17 in the Southside neighborhoods of Columbus, Ohio. We primarily serve low- and moderate-income families and focus on using sports as a platform for positive youth development, academic support, and community engagement.

Our core operations include seasonal youth sports leagues in soccer, basketball, flag football, baseball/softball, and volleyball. Each year we run:
- Spring and fall outdoor soccer leagues (approximately 28 teams per season across age brackets 6–8, 9–11, 12–14, and 15–17).
- Winter indoor basketball leagues (approximately 22 teams using rented school gymnasiums and a local recreation center).
- Summer evening baseball and softball programs (coed, with skills clinics and non-travel league play for about 16 teams).
- A smaller, coed flag football league in the fall (8–10 teams depending on registration numbers).

In addition to the leagues themselves, we operate:
- Skills development clinics and open gyms throughout the year (e.g., pre-season basketball skills camps, goalkeeper training for soccer, introductory clinics for kids who have never played organized sports).
- A volunteer coach training and certification program, which includes background checks, online abuse prevention training, concussion awareness modules, and in-person workshops focused on positive coaching, inclusive practices, and basic first aid.
- An after-school academic support and tutoring component that operates three days per week during the school year in partnership with two local middle schools. Youth participating in our leagues are encouraged to attend these sessions, where we provide homework help, literacy support, and study-skills workshops.
- Community events such as annual "Back-to-School Sports & Health Fair" (with free sports physicals, basic health screenings, and equipment giveaways) and a "Family Sports Day" each summer that features friendly games, parent-child scrimmages, and information booths from local health and social service providers.

Our events are typically held at public school fields and gymnasiums, Columbus Parks & Recreation facilities, and occasionally at privately owned indoor sports centers that we rent. We do not own any facilities. We coordinate all permits with the City of Columbus Recreation and Parks Department and Columbus City Schools, and we maintain liability insurance for facility use and participant activities.

We have a strong emphasis on safety and risk management. All volunteers and staff working directly with youth undergo annual background checks. We have written policies on supervision ratios, emergency response (including concussion protocols and heat safety guidelines), transportation (we do not provide routine transportation and discourage unsupervised carpooling; when we charter buses for special events, we use licensed carriers), and social media/photography consent. Incident reports are completed for any injury requiring more than basic first aid, and we review these reports quarterly at the board level to identify trends and areas for improvement.

Financially and programmatically, we are supported through a combination of registration fees (which are kept low and supplemented with need-based scholarships), small corporate sponsorships from local businesses, foundation grants, and individual donations. We work closely with neighborhood associations, churches, and schools to identify youth who might benefit from participation but may face financial or transportation barriers.

We do not operate any competitive travel teams; all of our programming is recreational or developmental in nature, with a focus on participation, skill-building, and character development rather than elite competition. We also do not engage in high-risk sports (e.g., tackle football, ice hockey, or extreme sports). Our highest-risk activities tend to be standard contact team sports (basketball, soccer) for which we follow nationally recognized safety standards.

Our board of directors is actively involved in governance, strategic planning, fundraising, and oversight of policies related to employment, volunteers, and youth protection. The organization employs a small full-time staff who handle day-to-day operations, supported by a large pool of community volunteers who serve as coaches, team parents, event helpers, and mentors.
